summary: Reading Circles feature testing completed successfully. Backend: 20/20 tests passed (100% success rate). Frontend: All UI components and navigation working correctly. No critical issues found.
critical_code_review_comments: All circle endpoints properly implemented with authentication checks
Member approval flow for private circles working correctly
Invite code generation uses secure random string (8 chars, uppercase + digits)
Member progress calculation includes streak and total chapters read
Frontend has proper testID attributes for all interactive elements
Circle detail screen correctly shows creator badge and member progress bars
Privacy modes (public/private) and plan modes (individual/shared) implemented correctly
seed_data_creation: Test circles created during pytest execution with TEST_ prefix. Existing circle 'Sunday School Group' with invite code B4PUN5E5 used for frontend testing.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Reading Circles feature fully tested and working. Backend has 100% test coverage for all circle endpoints. Frontend UI is complete with all modals, navigation, and detail screens working. Previous issues from iteration_1 (Plans screen rendering, Bible Map navigation) were not retested in this iteration as focus was on new Circles feature.

circles_backend: POST /api/circles - Create circle (public/private, individual/shared plan mode)
GET /api/circles - List user's circles with member count
GET /api/circles/{id} - Get circle detail with member progress bars
POST /api/circles/join - Join circle via invite code (active for public, pending for private)
GET /api/circles/invite/{code} - Get circle info by invite code (no auth required)
Invite code generation (8 chars, unique, uppercase + digits)
Member progress calculation (total chapters read, current streak)
Privacy enforcement (403 for non-members viewing private circles)
circles_frontend: Circles tab appears as 5th tab in bottom navigation
Circles screen with 'Reading Circles' title and subtitle
Create Circle button opens modal with all fields
Join Circle button opens modal with invite code input
Create Circle modal fields: name, description, privacy (public/private), plan mode (individual/shared)
Privacy toggles with icons (globe for public, lock for private)
Plan mode toggles with icons (person for individual, people for shared)
Circle cards display: name, description, privacy icon, member count
Circle cards are tappable and navigate to detail screen
Circle detail screen shows: circle name, privacy, member count, description
Invite code section with code display and Share button
Member Progress section with progress bars
Creator badge displayed for circle creator
Chapters read count for each member
Back navigation from detail to list

test_credentials_used.test_users: Generated dynamically during pytest with unique emails

no_mocked_apis: All APIs are real. No mocked endpoints.
rca_of_issue: N/A - No issues found during testing
